title: heptapleurum lanatum
intro: Heptapleurum lanatum, also known as the Himalayan quinine or Quinine tree, is a plant species native to the Himalayas, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimalarial effects. The health benefits of Heptapleurum lanatum include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and malaria, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Heptapleurum lanatum is typically prepared as a tincture or decoction for medicinal use, and its roots and bark are used in herbal preparations.
